messages.content: Hey guys. Victor, how's Mom? I called her Sunday and she seemed a little scattered. Kept repeating the same story about Tía Elena.
She has her moments. Overall she's doing well. Rosa is great with her and she sees Dr. Pham next week.
I visited last weekend! She was in great spirits. We looked through old photos and she remembered everything about the Guadalajara trip. She made me pozole 🥣
That's good to hear. I just worry because I'm so far away. Vic can you keep me in the loop after her appointment?
Of course. I'll let you know how it goes.
Mom's appointment went well. Dr. Pham says she's stable on the donepezil. No changes to treatment. She had a good day — knew everyone, told the nurse about the restaurant.
Thanks for the update. Stable is good. Does Dr. Pham think she'll need a higher level of care anytime soon?
Not at this time. She's well cared for at home.
That's wonderful news. Tía is tough. She's not going anywhere 💪
I'm thinking about coming for Thanksgiving. It's been too long since I've seen Mom in person.
That would be great Ale! I'm driving down from Chicago too. Full family Thanksgiving!
Sounds good. I'll be working lunch at the restaurant but I'll be home by 4. I'm doing the turkey.
I'll handle the tamales. Mom's recipe. I've been practicing.
Victor. Mom didn't know it was Thanksgiving today. She asked where Papá was. Twice.
She has confusion episodes. They come and go. Dr. Pham is aware.
Come and go? She called me Papa for ten minutes. She didn't know what year it was. That's not a bad day Victor. That's decline.
She did have a rough evening but she was fine this morning. We made breakfast together and she was totally with it. Please don't panic after one bad moment.
I'm not panicking. I'm being realistic. She needs to be reassessed. And we need to have a serious conversation about what happens when things get worse.
Things are under control, Ale. I'm here every day. I manage her medication, her appointments, her meals. What exactly do you think needs to change?
For starters, she needs a memory care evaluation. And we should be looking at facilities. Just in case. Not saying right now but we should have a plan.
She is not going to a facility. She lives in her home with her son. That IS the plan.
Both of you. Take a breath. Nobody is putting Tía anywhere. Let's just get through the weekend and talk about this calmly later.
Victor, I've been doing research. There are excellent memory care facilities in both Milwaukee and Phoenix. I'm not the enemy here.
Phoenix? You want to move our mother 1,700 miles away from everything she knows? From her home, her church, her aide who she loves?
I want her to have options. And I want some transparency about her care. You won't share her records, you won't share Dr. Pham's notes, you won't even tell me the details of her medication changes.
There haven't been medication changes. She's stable on donepezil. I've told you this multiple times.
Then share the records and prove it. If everything is fine, what are you hiding?
Guys. Please. It's Christmas season. Can we not do this right now? Ale, Victor is doing his best. Victor, Ale has a right to be concerned. Can we find a middle ground?
I've tried middle ground for months. If Victor won't cooperate, I may need to look into power of attorney to make sure Mom's interests are protected.
Power of attorney. Wow. You're going to threaten me with legal action over our mother.
It's not a threat. It's an option. One you're forcing me toward by refusing to communicate.
I can't believe this is where we are. We're a family. Tía wouldn't want this. Both of you need to step back.

Victor, I'm done asking nicely. I've tried for months to have a conversation about Mom's care. If you won't even consider options, I'll have to take action. I'm telling you directly before I do anything.
I've given you updates. Mom is stable. I don't know what else you want from me.
I want her medical records. I want her financial records. I want to know exactly what's happening.
Talk to my attorney if you need records. Sandra Milovich, (414) 555-0617.
Your attorney. Unbelievable.
Victor, was that really necessary? Ale, please try to understand — Victor is overwhelmed. He's doing this alone.
He doesn't have to do it alone. That's the whole point.
I talked to Tía today. She asked about both of you. She said "my boys are fighting" and it made her cry. Just thought you should know.
I've retained an attorney. Douglas Hartfield. I didn't want to go this route but Victor has given me no choice. If you won't share Mom's records voluntarily, my attorney will handle it.
Ale, no. Please. Lawyers? We're family. There has to be another way.
I've tried every other way. Victor won't talk to me, won't share information, won't let me be involved. What am I supposed to do?
Victor? Are you there?
Victor, please say something. Don't shut down.
I have nothing to say that won't be used against me.
Used against you? Victor, we're your family. Nobody is trying to use anything against anyone. Please.
I called Mom today. She had a good day. Told me about the garden she wants to plant. I miss her.
She told me the same thing! She wants tomatoes and jalapeños. Like Abuela's garden.
Victor I sent you a formal records request. I expect you or your attorney to respond within 14 days.
My attorney has it. She'll respond to yours.
This is so sad. Tía deserves better than this from both of you.
I'm flying in Thursday the 20th. Arriving around noon. I'll come by the house to see Mom. Victor, I'd appreciate it if you were there.
I work Thursday lunch. I can be home by 3. Rosa will be with Mom until then.
I'll drive down from Chicago that morning. We can all have coffee and talk like adults. Please. For Tía.
Mom looked good today. She was happy to see me. We sat in the kitchen and she told me about the fish fry at church. She remembered everything about it.
I'm so glad. How did the visit go otherwise? Did you guys talk?
Victor and I were polite. That's about all I can say. He wouldn't discuss records or care plans. Told me to talk to his lawyer.
At least you saw Tía. That matters. Both of you are coming from a place of love even if it doesn't feel like it right now.
Mom had a good day with everyone here. That's what matters.
Morning Vic. How are we today?
Good day. Busy but good. Fall menu launched at the restaurant and Mom had a great morning.
Glad to hear it. 2 and a half years tomorrow, brother. That's something to be proud of.
913 days. Not that I'm counting.
You better be counting. Every one of them matters. See you Saturday?
Wouldn't miss it.
How's the week been? Any tough moments?
All good. Wine tasting at the restaurant yesterday for the fall list, but I had Maria handle it. Didn't even go near the room.
Smart man. Boundaries are everything. Proud of you.
Checking in. How's things?
Family stuff. Brother's coming for Thanksgiving and there's tension about Mom's care. He thinks I'm not doing enough.
Family can be the hardest trigger. You know that. How are you feeling about it?
Angry mostly. He doesn't do anything for Mom but has opinions about everything. I'm the one here every day.
Anger is dangerous for us. You know where to put it. Meeting Wednesday. Write about it before then.
Dave. Rough Thanksgiving. My brother saw Mom confused and went off about facilities and moving her to Phoenix. We argued. Badly.
I'm sorry brother. That's heavy. How are you right now? Be honest.
Cravings were real for the first time in months. Sitting in the kitchen and there's a bottle of Barolo on the counter from dinner. I can smell it from here.
Put the bottle away. Not in the trash, just out of sight. Pour yourself some water. Did you take your pill today?
Yes. 8 AM like always. I'm putting the bottle in the pantry. I'm not going to drink it.
I know you're not. But call me if that changes. Even at 3 AM. I mean it Vic.
I know. Thank you Dave.
Holiday season at the restaurant this week? How's that going?
It's nonstop. Had to pour champagne for 60 people at a corporate event last night. Maria handled the bar but I was running the floor and I could smell everything.
December is always the hardest month for people in our shoes. How'd you handle it?
Stayed busy. Focused on the food, the guests, the logistics. Didn't let myself stop moving. By the time I got home I was too tired to think about it.
That works short term. Make sure you're not just white-knuckling through December. Meeting Wednesday, journal before then. Deal?
Deal.

NYE was brutal. Open bar for the prix fixe, every table had bottles of champagne. I had to give a toast to the room at midnight with sparkling water in a champagne flute. Nobody noticed but I noticed.
But you're texting me at 1 AM sober. That's a win, Vic. Happy New Year brother.
Morning. How's the new year treating you?
Something's going on at work that I can't really talk about yet. Just stressed. Can't get into details.
You don't have to tell me what it is. Just tell me how you're handling it.
Taking my pill. Going to meetings. Not drinking. Just grinding through it.
That's all you can do. You know where I am.
Furnace died last night. -12 outside. Had to wrap Mom in every blanket in the house until the repair guy came this morning. $480 I didn't have.
Damn. Is she okay? Are you okay?
She's fine. I'm fine. Just tired of everything being an emergency.
I hear you. But you handled it. That's what matters.
How's the week?
Money's tight. Everything is tight. Legal bills, Mom's care costs, the restaurant needs things, and my brother is making everything harder.
Financial stress is one of the biggest relapse triggers. Are you doing okay? Really okay?
I'm holding it together. Barely. But I am.
Barely counts. Extra meeting this week? I'll go with you.
Yeah. Wednesday. I'll be there.
Valentine's Day at the restaurant tonight. 240 covers. Every table had wine and cocktails flowing. I'm exhausted.
How'd you do?
Fine. Stayed in the kitchen mostly. Tony and Maria ran the floor and bar. I just cooked and counted plates.
Smart. Knowing your limits is everything. Sleep well brother.
3 years today brother. I know you don't need me to tell you, but I'm proud of you. Meeting tonight if you want. Or just call.
Thanks Dave. It means everything. I'll be at the Wednesday meeting.
Morning brother. How'd you sleep?
3 hours. Mom was up at 2 AM confused. Thought she was at her parents' house. Took 45 minutes to get her settled.
That's rough. You take your pill this morning?
Yeah. 8 AM like clockwork. One thing I never miss.
Good man. Any tough moments this week?
Had a customer chew out my bartender about a cocktail. Old fashioned wasn't sweet enough or something. Stood there smelling bourbon for 10 minutes while I handled it. I'm fine but it wasn't nothing.
That's an honest answer. You want to talk before Saturday's meeting?
Nah I'm okay. Just wanted to name it. I'll see you Saturday.
Always here. 3 years and 6 days, Vic. That's not nothing either.
Still at the restaurant. Long night. Just tired.
Tired or struggling? No judgment.
Just tired. I think. Going home.
Text me when you're home safe. I mean it.
Home. Mom's asleep. I'm going to bed.
Proud of you. See you Saturday. Sleep well brother.
Hey boss. Fall menu is looking great. Pumpkin ravioli sold out by 8 last night. Want me to double prep tomorrow?
Double it. If we sell out two nights in a row that's a signal. Also bump up the short rib pappardelle — that's moving fast too.
On it. How's your Mom doing? You mentioned she had a doctor's visit this week.
She's good. Doctor says stable. Appreciate you asking, Tony.
Sysco was short on the wild mushroom blend. Had to sub with cremini and shiitake from Metro Market. Worked fine but wanted you to know.
Good call. I'll talk to Dan about the reliability issues. Third short shipment this month.
Tony, holiday party season. I need you to build out the prix fixe and get specialty orders to Sysco by next Friday. Can you handle the wine pairings with Maria?
Already on it. Meeting with Maria tomorrow to finalize pairings. I'm thinking the braised short rib, lobster ravioli, and chocolate torte for the prix fixe. Classic Bella Notte.
Perfect. That's exactly what I was thinking. Let's kill it this December.
NYE prep list is done. 120 covers confirmed for the prix fixe. Sysco special order confirmed for Dec 30. Extra oysters, lobster tails, and the good truffle oil. We're ready.
You're the best Tony. Seriously. I couldn't run this place without you.
How was your Christmas? How's your Mom?
Quiet. Just me and Mom. She had a good day. We watched old movies and ate tamales. It was nice.
Heads up — walk-in compressor sounded rough today. Temps are holding but I want to keep an eye on it. Might need a tech this week.
Good catch. I'll schedule a tech for this week. Keep logging temps twice daily until they come out.
Health inspection early notice came in. Late March or early April. Want me to start the deep clean rotation?
Yes. Start with the hood system and exhaust filters. We got 96 last time — I want to beat it. Let's be audit-ready by March 1.
Mom's appointment is the 30th right? Need me to drive her again?
Yeah, if you can. Appointment is at 10:30. I've got the owner call at 2 and can't make both.
Done. I'll pick her up at 9:45. She likes stopping at Culver's on the way back. My treat.
You spoil her. Thanks Tony.
Fish fry prep for Week 1 is dialed in. 70 portions of beer-battered cod, coleslaw, fries, mac and cheese. Luis is handling the fry station.
Great. Fr. Nowak says they might need more next week if word gets out. Be ready to scale to 85.
Valentine's Day was insane. 240 covers, no major mistakes, kitchen ran clean. Staff meal tomorrow to celebrate?
Absolutely. You earned it. The whole team did. I'll buy the steaks.
Hey boss. We're short on halibut, Sysco only sent 8 portions instead of 20. Want me to order from US Foods or just 86 it after tonight?
Order from US Foods, rush delivery. We've got that 8-top Thursday that pre-ordered the halibut special. Can't 86 it.
On it. Keisha at US Foods says she can get it to us by tomorrow 10 AM.
Perfect. Hey, any chance you could drive Mom to Dr. Pham's on Thursday? I've got the owner call at 2.
Of course. Same as last time? UW Health?
Yeah. Appointment's at 1:30. Thanks man. She likes your car better than mine anyway.
Ha. It's the heated seats. I'll pick her up at 12:45.
Health inspection confirmed for April 1. Need the deep clean scheduled this weekend.
Already on it. Marco and Ricky are doing the walk-in Saturday morning. I'll handle the line and hoods Sunday before service.
What would I do without you.
Hey Carmen, I'm closing tonight. Can you stay with Mom until I get home? Should be by 11:30.
Of course. I'll head over around 5:30. Does she need dinner or did you already cook?
Arroz con pollo in the fridge, just needs warming up. Meds are on the counter — donepezil at 8.
She ate everything. We watched novelas and she fell asleep on the couch around 9:30. Moved her to bed. All good here.
Just got home. Thank you Carmen. Seriously.
Closing again tonight. She had a good day — was singing in the kitchen when I left. Same routine with meds.
Love it when she sings. I'll be there by 5:30.
Victor, she got confused around 9 and asked for your dad. I just sat with her and rubbed her back until she calmed down. She's sleeping now.
Thank you for handling that. It happens sometimes. She always settles if someone just sits with her.
Are you closing Friday? I can come stay with your mom if you need.
Yeah, Friday and Saturday this week. Friday would be huge if you can.
Done. I'll bring some of that tres leches she liked last time. She kept talking about it for three days.
Carmen, big favor. Private event at the restaurant tonight — might not be home until midnight. Can you stay late?
Of course. Want me to handle the 8 o'clock meds?
Yes please. Donepezil at 8. Melatonin is on her nightstand if she can't sleep.
All quiet. She's been asleep since 9:15. I made her chamomile tea and she went right down. Take your time getting home.
Closing tonight. Furnace repair guy might still be there when you arrive — just let him finish. Mom's in the living room with extra blankets.
I'm here. Repair guy just left. House is warming back up. Made her hot chocolate. She's doing fine.
Home. House is warm again. You're a lifesaver Carmen.
Can you stay with Mom tonight? Closing again.
Of course. How was she today?
Great day. Rosa said she was in a wonderful mood — they baked cookies together. Should be an easy night.
She had a lovely evening. We looked at old photos and she told me about your dad's fishing trips up north. Her memory was really sharp tonight, Victor.
Closing tonight. She was a little off today — Rosa said some confusion this afternoon. Just keep things calm and quiet.
Got it. I'm here now. She seems okay — we're watching Wheel of Fortune and she just solved three puzzles before the contestants. I think she's doing fine.
Sandra, Alejandro just sent another email about Mom's records. Forwarding now.
Got it. Don't respond to him directly. I'll draft something.
He's now talking about power of attorney in the family group chat. Is that something he can actually pursue?
Not unilaterally. Your mother executed the POA while competent. It's valid. I'll walk you through it on Thursday's call.
CK sent another denial on the billing dispute. Same boilerplate as last time.
Forward it to me. I'll escalate to their compliance department. They can't keep stonewalling on this.
Can we move Thursday's call to 3? Mom has a doctor appointment at 1.
3 works. I'll have updates on the CK complaint and the Hartfield response by then.
Alejandro retained an attorney. Douglas Hartfield. He just announced it in the group chat.
I know Doug. He's reasonable. This may actually help — easier to negotiate attorney to attorney than through a family group text. Don't engage with Alejandro about legal matters anymore.
Victor, I sent a letter to Hartfield's office today. Formal response to Alejandro's records request. I've offered a supervised review with conditions. That should bring the temperature down.
Thank you Sandra. I just want this to go away.
I know. But doing it right protects both you and Lucia. Trust the process.
CK finally responded to the escalation. They're offering a partial credit on three months of overbilling. Not enough, but it's movement. Call me when you can.
I'll call after the lunch rush. Probably around 3.
Good morning Victor, I'm here. Lucia had breakfast already — oatmeal and tea. She was up early today.
Thank you Rosa. I left for the restaurant at 7. Let me know if she needs anything.
She's having a wonderful day. We walked out to the garden and she remembered all the rosebush names. She told me her mother planted them.
That makes my day. Thank you for taking her outside — she loves the garden.
Victor, small issue — she couldn't find her glasses this morning and got pretty agitated. Found them in the bathroom behind the soap dispenser. All settled now.
Sorry about that. I'll make sure they're on her nightstand before I leave. Thank you for being patient with her.
Good morning. She woke up in a great mood today. She's telling me all about the church Christmas party. We're going to make paper snowflakes.
Ha — she loves that. There should be construction paper in the hall closet. Thank you Rosa.
I'll be about 15 minutes late tomorrow — doctor appointment first thing in the morning. Is that okay?
Of course. I don't leave for the restaurant until 9:30 on Fridays so I'll be here. Take your time.
Victor, I noticed my schedule hasn't changed but CK told me the rate went up again? I'm confused. I thought you should know.
I appreciate you telling me Rosa. I'm looking into it with my attorney. It's not something you need to worry about — your care has been wonderful and that's not changing.
Lucia had a rough afternoon. She got upset after lunch and kept asking when you were coming home. I sat with her and we went through the photo album. She calmed down after about twenty minutes.
Thank you Rosa. I hate that I can't always be there when that happens. You handle those moments better than anyone.
She's lucky to have you, Victor. I'll see you both Monday. Have a good weekend.
Hi Victor, is there any way I could swap my Friday PM for a morning shift earlier in the week? Wednesday or Thursday would work.
Sure, I can make that work. I'll update the schedule.
Thank you so much. I really appreciate it.
Victor, could I take Tuesday instead of Friday this week? Sorry for the short notice.
That's fine Jessica. I'll move you.
Hi Victor, any chance I could switch to mornings only for the next couple weeks? I'm happy to pick up extra Saturday AMs to make up for it.
Of course. Let me know if you need anything else.
Thank you so much for being flexible with my schedule. I really appreciate it.
